medical_history.chief_complaint: Widespread lymphadenopathy and hepatosplenomegaly with associated fevers and night sweats.
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The patient presented for investigation; symptoms began with lymphadenopathy and hepatosplenomegaly, and worsened over time with the addition of fevers and night sweats. Painful, pigmented subcutaneous nodules developed on her legs after 2 cycles of chemotherapy.
medical_history.social_history: Non-smoker; occasional alcohol consumption; works in a sedentary office job.

physical_examination.general_examination: The patient exhibited widespread lymphadenopathy and hepatosplenomegaly. Notable tender, pigmented subcutaneous nodules consistent with erythema nodosum were present on both legs. Neurological examination revealed no focal deficits; the patient was alert and oriented.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Biopsy of a typical shin nodule showed inflammation within subcutaneous fat (consistent with erythema nodosum) with no evidence of lymphomatous infiltrate. The significant lymphadenopathy and hepatosplenomegaly suggested systemic involvement.
